title: Radius and diameter
description: Practice finding the radius and diameter of a circle using both vocabulary and visuals.

assessment_items.question_content:
The radius of a circle is $3$ units. 

**What is the diameter of the circle?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units

The radius of a circle is $2$ units. 

**What is the diameter of the circle?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units

The radius of a circle is $8$ units. 

**What is the diameter of the circle?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units

The radius of a circle is $5$ units. 

**What is the diameter of the circle?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units

The radius of a circle is $1$ units. 

**What is the diameter of the circle?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units

The diameter of a circle is $6$ units. 

**What is the radius of the circle?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units

The diameter of a circle is $4$ units. 

**What is the radius of the circle?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units

The diameter of a circle is $16$ units. 

**What is the radius of the circle?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units

The diameter of a circle is $10$ units. 

**What is the radius of the circle?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units

The diameter of a circle is $2$ units. 

**What is the radius of the circle?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units

**What is the radius and diameter of the following circle?**

[[☃ image 1]]

Radius $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$

Diameter $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
$\purpleD{\text{Radius}}$ is a straight line that connects the center of a circle to a point on the circumference (outside of the circle).


$\greenD{\text{Diameter}}$ is a straight line that goes through the center of a circle and connects two points on the circumference (outside of the circle).


The line shown goes across the entire circle, so it is the $\greenD{\text{diameter}}$. 

$\greenD{\text{Diameter}}=\greenD{18} \text{ cm}$
The radius of a circle is half the length of the diameter.  


$\begin{align}\purpleD{r}&=\dfrac{\greenD{d}}2
 \\\\
\purpleD{r}&=\dfrac{\greenD{18}}2 
\\\\
\purpleD{r}&=\purpleD9\text{ cm} \end{align}$
$\text{\purpleD{Radius}}=\purpleD9\text{ cm}$

$\greenD{\text{Diameter}}=\greenD{18} \text{ cm}$

options.alt: A circle with a line segment passing through the centre, labelled as 18 cm, connects two opposite points on the boundary of the circle.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the radius and diameter of the following circle?**

[[☃ image 1]]

Radius $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$

Diameter $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
$\purpleD{\text{Radius}}$ is a straight line that connects the center of a circle to a point on the circumference (outside of the circle).


$\greenD{\text{Diameter}}$ is a straight line that goes through the center of a circle and connects two points on the circumference (outside of the circle).


The line shown goes across the entire circle, so it is the $\greenD{\text{diameter}}$. 

$\greenD{\text{Diameter}}=\greenD{14} \text{ cm}$
The radius of a circle is half the length of the diameter.  


$\begin{align}\purpleD{r}&=\dfrac{\greenD{d}}2
 \\\\
\purpleD{r}&=\dfrac{\greenD{14}}2 
\\\\
\purpleD{r}&=\purpleD7\text{ cm} \end{align}$
$\text{\purpleD{Radius}}=\purpleD7\text{ cm}$

$\greenD{\text{Diameter}}=\greenD{14} \text{ cm}$

options.alt: A circle with a line segment passing through the centre, labelled as 14 cm, connects two opposite points on the boundary of the circle.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the radius and diameter of the following circle?**

[[☃ image 1]]

Radius $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$

Diameter $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
$\purpleD{\text{Radius}}$ is a straight line that connects the center of a circle to a point on the circumference (outside of the circle).


$\greenD{\text{Diameter}}$ is a straight line that goes through the center of a circle and connects two points on the circumference (outside of the circle).


The line shown goes across the entire circle, so it is the $\greenD{\text{diameter}}$. 

$\greenD{\text{Diameter}}=\greenD{7} \text{ cm}$
The radius of a circle is half the length of the diameter.  


$\begin{align}\purpleD{r}&=\dfrac{\greenD{d}}2
 \\\\
\purpleD{r}&=\dfrac{\greenD{7}}2 
\\\\
\purpleD{r}&=\purpleD{3.5}\text{ cm} \end{align}$
$\text{\purpleD{Radius}}=\purpleD{3.5}\text{ cm}$

$\greenD{\text{Diameter}}=\greenD{7} \text{ cm}$

options.alt: A circle with a line segment passing vertically through its centre. The line segment connects two opposite points on the boundary of the circle. The line segment is labelled as 7 cm.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the radius and diameter of the following circle?**


[[☃ image 1]]

Radius $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$

Diameter $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
$\purpleD{\text{Radius}}$ is a straight line that connects the center of a circle to a point on the circumference (outside of the circle).


$\greenD{\text{Diameter}}$ is a straight line that goes through the center of a circle and connects two points on the circumference (outside of the circle).


The line shown goes across the entire circle, so it is the $\greenD{\text{diameter}}$. 

$\greenD{\text{Diameter}}=\greenD{24} \text{ cm}$
The radius of a circle is half the length of the diameter.  


$\begin{align}\purpleD{r}&=\dfrac{\greenD{d}}2
 \\\\
\purpleD{r}&=\dfrac{\greenD{24}}2 
\\\\
\purpleD{r}&=\purpleD12\text{ cm} \end{align}$
$\text{\purpleD{Radius}}=\purpleD{12}\text{ cm}$

$\greenD{\text{Diameter}}=\greenD{24} \text{ cm}$

options.alt: A circle with a line segment passing through the centre, labelled as 24 cm, connects two opposite points on the boundary of the circle.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the radius and diameter of the following circle?**

[[☃ image 1]]

Radius $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$

Diameter $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
$\purpleD{\text{Radius}}$ is a straight line that connects the center of a circle to a point on the circumference (outside of the circle).


$\greenD{\text{Diameter}}$ is a straight line that goes through the center of a circle and connects two points on the circumference (outside of the circle).


The line shown goes across the entire circle, so it is the $\greenD{\text{diameter}}$. 

$\greenD{\text{Diameter}}=\greenD{13} \text{ cm}$
The radius of a circle is half the length of the diameter.  


$\begin{align}\purpleD{r}&=\dfrac{\greenD{d}}2
 \\\\
\purpleD{r}&=\dfrac{\greenD{13}}2 
\\\\
\purpleD{r}&=\purpleD{6.5}\text{ cm} \end{align}$
$\text{\purpleD{Radius}}=\purpleD{6.5}\text{ cm}$

$\greenD{\text{Diameter}}=\greenD{13} \text{ cm}$

options.alt: A circle with a line segment passing through the centre, labelled as 13 cm, connects two opposite points on the boundary of the circle.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the radius and diameter of the following circle?**


[[☃ image 1]]

Radius $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$

Diameter $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
$\purpleD{\text{Radius}}$ is a straight line that connects the center of a circle to a point on the circumference (outside of the circle).


$\greenD{\text{Diameter}}$ is a straight line that goes through the center of a circle and connects two points on the circumference (outside of the circle).

The line shown goes from the center to the outside of the circle, so it is the $\purpleD{\text{radius}}$. 

$\purpleD{\text{Radius}}=\purpleD{5.1} \text{ cm}$

The diameter of a circle is twice the length of the radius.  

$\begin{align}\greenD{d}&=2\purpleD{r}
 \\\\
\greenD{d}&=2\times5.1 
\\\\
\greenD{d}&=\greenD{10.2}\text{ cm} \end{align}$

options.alt: A circle with a line segment, labelled as 5.1 cm, connects the centre of the circle to the boundary of the circle.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the radius and diameter of the following circle?**


[[☃ image 1]]

Radius $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$

Diameter $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
$\purpleD{\text{Radius}}$ is a straight line that connects the center of a circle to a point on the circumference (outside of the circle).


$\greenD{\text{Diameter}}$ is a straight line that goes through the center of a circle and connects two points on the circumference (outside of the circle).



The line shown goes from the center to the outside of the circle, so it is the $\purpleD{\text{radius}}$. 

$\purpleD{\text{Radius}}=\purpleD{11} \text{ cm}$
The diameter of a circle is twice the length of the radius.  

$\begin{align}\greenD{d}&=2\purpleD{r}
 \\\\
\greenD{d}&=2\times11 
\\\\
\greenD{d}&=\greenD{22}\text{ cm} \end{align}$

$\text{\purpleD{Radius}}=\purpleD{11}\text{ cm}$

$\greenD{\text{Diameter}}=\greenD{22} \text{ cm}$

options.alt: A circle with a line segment, labelled as 11 cm, connects the centre of the circle to the boundary of the circle.
